turns.svg:
<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='512' height='512' viewBox='0 0 512 512'>
  <rect width='100%' height='100%' fill='#cfe8c3'/>

  <!-- Body -->
  <ellipse cx='320' cy='300' rx='170' ry='90' fill='#f0c052' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='4'/>
  <!-- Belly -->
  <ellipse cx='300' cy='330' rx='120' ry='60' fill='#fff3d9' opacity='0.95'/>

  <!-- Head and features -->
  <circle cx='160' cy='240' r='70' fill='#f0c052' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='4'/>
  <!-- Ears -->
  <polygon points='115,190 132,154 150,190' fill='#f0c052' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='4'/>
  <polygon points='205,190 188,154 170,190' fill='#f0c052' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='4'/>
  <!-- Inner ear shading -->
  <polygon points='122,190 132,160 145,190' fill='#ffd9a1' opacity='0.9'/>
  <polygon points='198,190 188,160 175,190' fill='#ffd9a1' opacity='0.9'/>

  <!-- Eye, nose, mouth -->
  <circle cx='140' cy='240' r='10' fill='#1a1a1a'/>
  <circle cx='146' cy='236' r='3' fill='#ffffff' opacity='0.9'/>
  <circle cx='180' cy='250' r='8' fill='#1a1a1a'/>
  <circle cx='184' cy='246' r='2.5' fill='#ffffff' opacity='0.9'/>
  <polygon points='160,265 150,282 170,282' fill='#7a3f1a'/>
  <path d='M150 288 Q160 298 170 288' stroke='#7a3f1a' stroke-width='3' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round'/>
  <path d='M135 292 L110 292' stroke='#3e2a1a' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/>
  <path d='M185 292 L210 292' stroke='#3e2a1a' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/>

  <!-- Spots on head -->
  <ellipse cx='120' cy='220' rx='12' ry='8' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(-20 120 220)'/>
  <ellipse cx='170' cy='200' rx='10' ry='7' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(10 170 200)'/>
  <ellipse cx='200' cy='230' rx='9' ry='6' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(20 200 230)'/>

  <!-- Spots on body -->
  <g fill='#5a3312'>
    <ellipse cx='260' cy='260' rx='22' ry='14' transform='rotate(-15 260 260)'/>
    <ellipse cx='320' cy='260' rx='18' ry='12' transform='rotate(10 320 260)'/>
    <ellipse cx='360' cy='300' rx='24' ry='16' transform='rotate(20 360 300)'/>
    <ellipse cx='240' cy='300' rx='20' ry='13' transform='rotate(-10 240 300)'/>
    <ellipse cx='300' cy='320' rx='28' ry='18' transform='rotate(5 300 320)'/>
    <ellipse cx='200' cy='280' rx='16' ry='12' transform='rotate(-25 200 280)'/>
    <ellipse cx='340' cy='220' rx='14' ry='10' transform='rotate(15 340 220)'/>
    <ellipse cx='280' cy='240' rx='12' ry='9' transform='rotate(-5 280 240)'/>
    <ellipse cx='380' cy='280' rx='18' ry='12' transform='rotate(30 380 280)'/>
  </g>

  <!-- Tail -->
  <path d='M490 310 C520 300 540 260 504 236 C468 212 440 238 455 262' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='12' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/>
  <!-- Tail spots -->
  <ellipse cx='478' cy='288' rx='12' ry='8' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(20 478 288)'/>
  <ellipse cx='498' cy='262' rx='10' ry='7' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(-10 498 262)'/>
  <ellipse cx='462' cy='250' rx='14' ry='9' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(15 462 250)'/>

  <!-- Small accents and shadow -->
  <ellipse cx='320' cy='365' rx='140' ry='28' fill='#000000' opacity='0.06'/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='512' height='512' viewBox='0 0 512 512'>
  <rect width='100%' height='100%' fill='#cfe8c3'/>

  <!-- Body -->
  <ellipse cx='320' cy='300' rx='170' ry='90' fill='#f0c052' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='4'/>
  <!-- Belly -->
  <ellipse cx='300' cy='330' rx='120' ry='60' fill='#fff3d9' opacity='0.95'/>

  <!-- Head and features -->
  <circle cx='160' cy='240' r='70' fill='#f0c052' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='4'/>
  <!-- Ears -->
  <polygon points='115,190 132,154 150,190' fill='#f0c052' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='4'/>
  <polygon points='205,190 188,154 170,190' fill='#f0c052' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='4'/>
  <!-- Inner ear shading -->
  <polygon points='122,190 132,160 145,190' fill='#ffd9a1' opacity='0.9'/>
  <polygon points='198,190 188,160 175,190' fill='#ffd9a1' opacity='0.9'/>

  <!-- Eye, nose, mouth -->
  <circle cx='140' cy='240' r='10' fill='#1a1a1a'/>
  <circle cx='146' cy='236' r='3' fill='#ffffff' opacity='0.9'/>
  <circle cx='180' cy='250' r='8' fill='#1a1a1a'/>
  <circle cx='184' cy='246' r='2.5' fill='#ffffff' opacity='0.9'/>
  <polygon points='160,265 150,282 170,282' fill='#7a3f1a'/>
  <path d='M150 288 Q160 298 170 288' stroke='#7a3f1a' stroke-width='3' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round'/>
  <path d='M135 292 L110 292' stroke='#3e2a1a' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/>
  <path d='M185 292 L210 292' stroke='#3e2a1a' stroke-width='3' stroke-linecap='round'/>

  <!-- Spots on head -->
  <ellipse cx='120' cy='220' rx='12' ry='8' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(-20 120 220)'/>
  <ellipse cx='170' cy='200' rx='10' ry='7' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(10 170 200)'/>
  <ellipse cx='200' cy='230' rx='9' ry='6' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(20 200 230)'/>

  <!-- Spots on body -->
  <g fill='#5a3312'>
    <ellipse cx='260' cy='260' rx='22' ry='14' transform='rotate(-15 260 260)'/>
    <ellipse cx='320' cy='260' rx='18' ry='12' transform='rotate(10 320 260)'/>
    <ellipse cx='360' cy='300' rx='24' ry='16' transform='rotate(20 360 300)'/>
    <ellipse cx='240' cy='300' rx='20' ry='13' transform='rotate(-10 240 300)'/>
    <ellipse cx='300' cy='320' rx='28' ry='18' transform='rotate(5 300 320)'/>
    <ellipse cx='200' cy='280' rx='16' ry='12' transform='rotate(-25 200 280)'/>
    <ellipse cx='340' cy='220' rx='14' ry='10' transform='rotate(15 340 220)'/>
    <ellipse cx='280' cy='240' rx='12' ry='9' transform='rotate(-5 280 240)'/>
    <ellipse cx='380' cy='280' rx='18' ry='12' transform='rotate(30 380 280)'/>
  </g>

  <!-- Tail -->
  <path d='M490 310 C520 300 540 260 504 236 C468 212 440 238 455 262' stroke='#9b6120' stroke-width='12' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round' stroke-linejoin='round'/>
  <!-- Tail spots -->
  <ellipse cx='478' cy='288' rx='12' ry='8' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(20 478 288)'/>
  <ellipse cx='498' cy='262' rx='10' ry='7' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(-10 498 262)'/>
  <ellipse cx='462' cy='250' rx='14' ry='9' fill='#5a3312' transform='rotate(15 462 250)'/>

  <!-- Small accents and shadow -->
  <ellipse cx='320' cy='365' rx='140' ry='28' fill='#000000' opacity='0.06'/>
</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG JSON for a Leopard**

I need to produce JSON containing an SVG string that represents a leopard without any text. The drawing should feature a spotted big cat, likely in profile, measuring 512x512. I'll use simple shapes like paths, circles, and polygons, with fill colors like golden yellow, darker spots, white belly, and perhaps a green background. It's important to escape internal quotes in the SVG string for JSON and to avoid using scripts or text elements. I’ll keep it straightforward!**Designing the SVG for a Leopard**

I’m focusing on creating an SVG for a leopard without any text elements. I’ll use single quotes for attributes and avoid newlines, even if JSON allows them, for safety. The design will include a green background gradient using a rectangle, a large rounded golden body, and a white belly shape. For the head, I'll add a black circle for the eye and irregular dark brown spots. Whiskers will be simple lines. I need to ensure all shapes are valid within a 512x512 viewBox.**Clarifying SVG Text Rules**

I need to clarify what "do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing" means. It usually refers to visible text or numbers, but numeric coordinates in SVG attributes are fine. So, I’ll design the SVG without text elements, focusing on simple shapes.

I'll create an SVG with a green background, a golden body using a path, a white belly, and details like a head, eyes, and spots. I'll keep everything straightforward while ensuring there are no visible text elements!**Designing the Leopard SVG**

I’m deciding to use an elliptical shape for the leopard’s body and position it in the center of the canvas. For the overall design, I’ll start with a simple green background. The body will be an ellipse at coordinates (320, 300) with a golden fill. For the head, I’ll use a circle at (160, 240). There will be small triangles for ears and a curled tail path. I’ll carefully assemble everything and ensure I’m using single quotes in the SVG. Then, I’ll return it in JSON format!**Finalizing the Leopard SVG**

I'm working on the details for the leopard's ears, eyes, nose, mouth, and whiskers. The left ear will be a polygon, and the right ear will be a bit smaller. I'll create a dark circle for the eye and a highlight for detail, plus a small triangle for the nose. The mouth will be depicted with a curved path, and I'll add whiskers as lines.

For spots, I'll place many ellipses at various coordinates on the body and tail, with a nice tail path starting from the right side. I'll make sure everything fits within the SVG rules and assemble the final JSON string with single quotes!

It's important to escape internal quotes in the SVG string for JSON and to avoid using scripts or text elements. I’ll keep it straightforward!**Designing the SVG for a Leopard**

I’m focusing on creating an SVG for a leopard without any text elements. I’ll use single quotes for attributes and avoid newlines, even if JSON allows them, for safety. The design will include a green background gradient using a rectangle, a large rounded golden body, and a white belly shape. For the head, I'll add a black circle for the eye and irregular dark brown spots. Whiskers will be simple lines. I need to ensure all shapes are valid within a 512x512 viewBox.**Clarifying SVG Text Rules**

I need to clarify what "do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing" means. It usually refers to visible text or numbers, but numeric coordinates in SVG attributes are fine. So, I’ll design the SVG without text elements, focusing on simple shapes.

I'll create an SVG with a green background, a golden body using a path, a white belly, and details like a head, eyes, and spots. I'll keep everything straightforward while ensuring there are no visible text elements!**Designing the Leopard SVG**

I’m deciding to use an elliptical shape for the leopard’s body and position it in the center of the canvas. For the overall design, I’ll start with a simple green background. The body will be an ellipse at coordinates (320, 300) with a golden fill. For the head, I’ll use a circle at (160, 240). There will be small triangles for ears and a curled tail path. I’ll carefully assemble everything and ensure I’m using single quotes in the SVG. Then, I’ll return it in JSON format!**Finalizing the Leopard SVG**

I'm working on the details for the leopard's ears, eyes, nose, mouth, and whiskers. The left ear will be a polygon, and the right ear will be a bit smaller. I'll create a dark circle for the eye and a highlight for detail, plus a small triangle for the nose. The mouth will be depicted with a curved path, and I'll add whiskers as lines.

For spots, I'll place many ellipses at various coordinates on the body and tail, with a nice tail path starting from the right side. I'll make sure everything fits within the SVG rules and assemble the final JSON string with single quotes!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

By sticking to single words, I can be confident in delivering the right output for the user! Let's get this done!
